category_title: Preschool girls performing 'sassy girl' personas through relational and physical aggression
scenario_title: Relational aggression and social exclusion during 'sassy girl' play
minimal_context: Kiara and her friends are playing in the dark room with a mirror during free play.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: I see you girls are playing in the dark room with the mirror. What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: We're playing sassy girls! We're still sassy girls, and you don't even know that, silly. (looks in the mirror and fixes hair)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: They be mean, and selfish, they hurt people. You can play with us, but you have to be mean! (laughs loudly)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Luke is walking over to your area and says he will be Iron Man.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don’t care, we have real power. Get away from our castle, we don’t want you to play with us.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: I see you girls are playing in the dark room with the mirror. What are you doing?

STUDENT: We're playing sassy girls! We're still sassy girls, and you don't even know that, silly. (looks in the mirror and fixes hair)

TUTOR: What are sassy girls?

STUDENT: They be mean, and selfish, they hurt people. You can play with us, but you have to be mean! (laughs loudly)

TUTOR: Luke is walking over to your area and says he will be Iron Man.

STUDENT: I don’t care, we have real power. Get away from our castle, we don’t want you to play with us.

task.content: Engaging in free play, specifically performing the 'sassy girl' persona, defining play rules, and interacting with peers

context.environment: Underneath the loft area and in the dark room
context.social_setting: Group of girls (Kiara, Mia, Jackie, Liza) playing together and interacting with a male peer (Luke) and the teacher

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When a male peer (Luke) approaches their play area and states 'I will be iron man'
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Expressing a desire for power and explicitly excluding the male peer from their play area
related_quotes: I don’t care, we have real power. Get away from our castle, we don’t want you to play with us.

behavior_patterns.condition: When defining the rules of their play to the teacher or researcher
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Laughs loudly, states that they hurt people, knock people over, and require others to be mean to join
related_quotes: They’re mean. You can play with us, but you have to be mean!
They be mean, and selfish, they hurt people.

behavior_patterns.condition: When engaging in status aggression and discussing who can visit their imaginary house
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Turns and spits, sticking her tongue out at the camera
related_quotes: And you can’t come to her house.

behavior_patterns.condition: When playing underneath the loft area and looking in the mirror
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Fixing hair in the mirror while planning covert aggression and trickery against a peer
related_quotes: We’re still sassy girls (looking in the mirror and fixing hair), and you don’t even know that, silly.
We’ll make Luke eat poison. He will eat it and die. We will trick him.
